title: Post's lattice with a schematic symbolic n-layer
description: A fixed-layout representation of the displayed Post lattice, enriched with definitions and generating bases transcribed from table tbl:posts_bases. Edges point from smaller to larger clones. The eight nodes with symbolic superscript n are schematic family members with n ≥ 4, placed between the unbounded member and degree 3; dashed chain edges indicate omitted intermediate degrees.

symbolicNLayer.placement: Each symbolic node is at the exact source-coordinate midpoint between its unbounded family member and its degree-3 member.
symbolicNLayer.reasonNoShiftWasNeeded: The original 2.5 source-unit gap provides 1.25 source units (56 browser pixels with the current transform) on each side of the inserted node.
symbolicNLayer.semanticCaveat: A symbolic n-node is a schematic representative of an arbitrary fixed family member, not one additional literal clone independent of n.

edgeMeaning.solid: Displayed cover/inclusion edge at one explicit layer.
edgeMeaning.dashed: A displayed gap in an infinite hierarchy; omitted intermediate degree layers may lie between the endpoints.

layoutNotes.detailsPlacement: Show definitions and bases in a tooltip or side panel rather than inside the circular node.

metadata.caption: List of all Boolean clones with their bases.

sources.metadataPolicy: The table is authoritative for definitions and bases. Fixed degree-2/degree-3 entries are obtained by instantiating its n-parametric rows.
